Section.name: UNCLASSIFIED
Section.Content: Prepared by: Johan Pauwels Date: 8/09/2023 Cegeka reference: PRD.0.L.136 DV1.1 CONFIDENTIAL Service Description PRD.0.L.136 TAM RedHat AMQ for Argenta Table of contents 1 Service Description 4 1.1 Service identification 4 1.2 Service summary 4 1.2.1 Technical Application Management 4 1.2.2 The Application 5 1.3 Service features 6 1.3.1 Standard Service features 6 1.3.1.1 Application knowledge 6 1.3.1.2 Technical setup 6 1.3.1.3 Technical management 6 1.3.1.4 Housekeeping 7 1.3.1.5 Monitoring 8 1.3.1.6 Security and Compliance 8 1.3.1.7 Backup and Restore 8 1.3.1.8 Incident and problem management 9 1.3.1.9 Customers responsibility 9 1.3.2 Optional Service features 9 1.3.3 Requests 10 1.3.4 Out of scope 11 1.4 Service characteristics 12 1.4.1 Obligatory 12 1.4.2 Cegeka owned Licenses and/or subscriptions 12 1.4.3 Customer owned Licenses and/or subscriptions 12 1.4.4 Conditions 12 1.4.5 Service delivery location 12 1.4.6 Service Reporting 13 1.4.7 Data classification 13 1.4.8 Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer 13 1.4.9 Miscellaneous 13 1.4.10 RACI Matrix 14 1.5 Adherence to Cross-Functional Services 16 2 Service Level Management 17 2.1 Service Window 17 2.2 Service Level 17 3 Service Billing 18 3.1 Standard Service Billing 18 3.2 Optional Service Billing 18 3.3 Service Request Billing 18 4 Document control 19 5 Document approval 20

Section.name: Service identification
Section.Content: Service TAM RedHat AMQ for Argenta Unique ID PRD.0.L.136 Service Model Technical Application Management (TAM) This service is dependant of other layers (IaaS and/or PaaS) which are indicated in the ‘Obligatory’ chapter of this document and which have their own Service Description. The combination of the layers assures the customer of the ‘infrastructure up to the technical application’ management. These obligatory services are called the ‘Platform’.

Section.name: Technical Application Management
Section.Content: The Service provides the tasks and activities needed to manage the RedHat AMQ application (The Application) from a technical perspective. Together with the underlaying obligatory Platform services, Cegeka unburdens the customer with all technical aspects from infrastructure up to the technical Application. The Standard Service features are focussed to keep the Service up-and-running, to keep it secure and to optimize its technical parameters. The Customer is responsible for the functional AMQ usage (usage of queues by business applications). The Customer will be able to use all functionalities of the Application. Cegeka ensures the functionalities of the Application.

Section.name: The Application
Section.Content: Red Hat AMQ is a messaging system that allows exchange of messages (data) between senders and receivers. A “broker” handles the exchange of the messages between sending and receiving clients. It is also capable of coordinating with other brokers, storing messages on persistent storage and the monitoring and management of the various components of the messaging system. Below a graphical overview of a typical set-up:

Section.name: Standard Service features
Section.Content: Cegeka ensures skilled engineers with experience on the RedHat AMQ technology in order to perform the Service. Cegeka advises in the setup of the underlaying platform in order to set it up according to the technology requirements of the Application. This may be different for each Customer environment (DTAP - Development, Test, Acceptance, Production and it’s variants). High availability setup: this setup advise includes also the setup of HA (High Available) environments where needed. Cegeka will advise a cost effective solution within the context of the available platform(s) of the Customer and the best practices of the Application. Cegeka performs and maintains the technical installation of the Application on the agreed platform ensuring its correct working. Technical setup is documented on operational level and is available to the Customer. Cegeka is responsible for the technical operation of the Application and to assure it’s correct running and availability of the platform functionalities (queues) during the agreed availability windows. This includes operational activities such as creating and deleting queue’s, assigning users and groups, etc. This applies to the Broker configuration. Queues/ACL provisioning The Service deals with the creation/deletion/modification of queues and ACL’s and maintenance of the existing ones in the broker, based upon the content and requirements, provided by the functional business application manager. Cegeka as technical application managers ensure that the queues and ACL’s are provisioned on the systems following the best practices in place. The Customer is responsible for configuring the connections and parameters in the applications. For systems in scope of Cegeka management, the clients (or libraries) are updated and configured per that service (i.e., JBoss). Usage of message queuing How the applications connect to this Service and the application components responsible for that, are not in scope of this Service. Cegeka does provide guidance on the required configuration and parameters for these clients. Clients that are controlled by Cegeka are covered in their respective services. This applies for instance to Linux servers or containers running JBoss (the JBoss application management ensures that the connection is implemented and maintained). Cegeka assists in providing best practices for connection settings and on request can force specific parameters to align with customer security requirements. Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Application. Automation is applied where possible. Proactive management Housekeeping includes following regular checks: Resolution actions are triggered when needed and followed to assure the SLA. Patching The service includes a maximum of 4 patch cycles (per environment) per year for the managed Application (inclusive the system database(s) if applicable). Patching is done in accordance to the operational Patch Plan. Additional patching can be done on RFC basis. Patching (includes minor updates) of the underlying platform is part of the Service of that platform. Life Cycle Management Cegeka monitors the Technology version of the service and notifies the Customer in accordance with the agreed LCM policy (see cross-functional Services) when used versions are subject to end-of-support or end-of-life. Appropriate actions (updates, replacement or other actions) are planned in accordance with agreed maintenance windows and will be executed on T&M basis in case of major updates. Patches and minor updates are included in the service cost as part of ‘Patching’. Cegeka monitors the correct working of the Application on all Customer environments. Monitoring improvements will be done during the lifecycle of the Service and are based on evaluation of incidents, problem management or other suggestions. The underlaying platform is also monitored according to the related Service Description of the platform. Monitoring events are evaluated during the Service Window in accordance to the Service Level of the individual environment. Notable events are treated as Incident tickets and are handled according to regular incident management. Management of technical users User and Access Management is limited to configuring and maintaining a connection to a central authentication source (LDAP/AD) available in the environment of the customer. Assigning groups and ACL’s to queue’s is performed during creation of a queue and can be changed/updated during the lifetime of an ACL/Queue through the standard change process (in an automated fashion). Administering users and groups (i.e., creating a new user or group) in the central LDAP or AD environment is not part of this Service. Detection of security incidents The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies. Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders. SIEM Additional monitoring can be done based on System and/or Application logs, which can be forwarded to a Customer SIEM solution (Security reason) or other solution (compliance reason, e.g. central collection of application logs to oversee the application stack) on request. Underlaying Platform hardening The underlaying platform and OS will be hardened as part of and according to the Service Description of the related Services. The operational Backup Plan contains the information on frequency and retention of the backups in accordance to the Customer backup policy. Restores can be done both on file and on full system level. These can be requested via the regular RFC process. Duration of a restore is dependant of the request (file or full; size of the item(s) to be restored). Application settings are subject to the backup of the underlaying platform. Besides monitoring incidents (see ‘Monitoring’) also other incidents can be reported by both the Customer and Cegeka. These will be subject to the regular incident management process (See Cross-functionals). Related to incident management, Cegeka performs also problem management (See Cross-functionals). The functional usage and monitoring of the Application is the responsibility of the Customer.

Section.name: Optional Service features
Section.Content: This section describes the features that can be optionally added to the standard Service features and that will be priced (when applicable) on top of the standard Service features. The following optional features are possible with the Service.

Section.name: Requests
Section.Content: Both the Customer and Cegeka can issue a Request for Change (RFC) or a Request for Information (RFI). There are multiple types of requests: The handling of Requests is subject to the regular change management process (see Cross-functionals). request type Description Lead time Service Requests AMQ management i.e. demand a new, change on or removal of an AMQ queue 2 working days Processing a multi-item and environment request i.e., Based on pseudo-code: add, modify or delete a queue, acl or user for multiple environments (DTAP) of the same queue 2 working days Normal Changes Any other request. Examples: Zero-day patching Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request.

Section.name: Out of scope
Section.Content: Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope of this Service. Specifically:

Section.name: Obligatory
Section.Content: The following services are mandatory for the delivery of this service:

Section.name: Cegeka owned Licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: The licenses and/or subscriptions listed in this section and their cost are included in this Service. It is Cegeka’s responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Licenses and/or subscriptions used for Cross-Functional Services are included in the Service and are not listed below. License description/remarks (no Cegeka owned licenses) -

Section.name: Customer owned Licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: The licenses and/or subscriptions listed in this section and their cost are not included in this Service. It is the customers responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Cegeka will report & advise on demand regarding license management. License description/remarks Redhat AMQ

Section.name: Conditions
Section.Content: It is mandatory to use the License Compliance platform to be able to report on installed software

Section.name: Service delivery location
Section.Content: This Service is delivered remotely from the Cegeka (virtual) Office environment.

Section.name: Service Reporting
Section.Content: The Cross-functional reporting is part of this Service Reporting where it is applicable for this service (see section ‘Relation to Cross-Functional Services’). More advanced reporting can be provided when on-boarding the Red AMQ environment in the Dynatrace APM environment. In that case specific dashboards, reporting and follow up on application level can be provided. This is handled through the Dynatrace APM service and is not included in this service.

Section.name: Data classification
Section.Content: Cegeka requires no access to confidentional and/or personal data to perform this Service. Engineer credentials do not need to access such data for the purpose of this Service. In case of assistance in investigations of functional issues, Cegeka personnel may see or receive fragments of such data in case it is shared by the Customer (data fragments in the sense of exports, screen sharing, screen shots, etc. for investigation purposes).

Section.name: Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer
Section.Content: This service is delivered with: Subcontractor (Cegeka manages the Service with own engineers which are supported by an external party for escalations or technology specific questions or issues): RedHat Sub-outsourcer (Cegeka delegates the Service to an external party): (none)

Section.name: Miscellaneous
Section.Content: n/a

Section.name: RACI Matrix
Section.Content: Activity Cegeka Customer Technical Application Management Maintain technical Application knowledge A,R Installing Application software A,R C Technical configuring the Application software (Create, Delete and Modify brokers) A,R C Maintain technical Application documentation A,R C Identifying technical Application errors and problems A,R C Technical troubleshooting Application errors and incidents A,R C Technical performance tuning analysis A,R C System parameterization for technical performance (tuning) A,R C Creating and/or restoring backups A,R C Make changes to system configuration A,R C Configure backups A,R C Perform backups and restores, assuring correct backup A,R C Housekeeping Follow up patches and upgrades of the application A,R C Evaluate and assess patch and upgrade effect on infrastructure A,R C Technical testing of the Application after deployment of patches and upgrades A,R C Validation of functional usage after deploying patches and upgrades C A,R Monitoring and event management Monitor and report technical application errors and problems based on logs provided by the application A,R C Monitor capacity (CPU, memory, storage, network, ...) of the underlaying platform A,R C License Management Buy licenses I A,R Assign / Configure licenses A,R C Report on license usage on the Application A,R I Functional Application Usage Maintain functional Application knowledge A,R Using the functional application software C,I A,R Validation of functional usage after deploying patches and upgrades C A,R RACI role definition:

Section.name: Adherence to Cross-Functional Services
Section.Content: The Service described in this Service Description adheres to the Cross-functional Services as described in the contract where this Service is residing unless documented in the table below: Cross-Functional Service Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services Anti-Malware management This service is not subject to Anti-Malware management. Anti-Malware management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. Vulnerability management & compliancy management (hardening) This service is not subject to vulnerability & compliancy management. Vulnerability & compliancy management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. All Cross -Functional services rely on the Cegeka standard framework for processes and tooling.

Section.name: Service Window
Section.Content: The Service Window is the time frame during which the agreed support services from Cegeka are available. Service windows are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract.

Section.name: Service Level
Section.Content: Service Levels of the cross-functional services are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract. Availability The following Availability KPI’s are provided for the Service: KPI ID KPI name Availability Service Window Metric KPI.0.136.Bronze TAM Bronze Bronze % availability in Service Window KPI.0.136.Silver TAM Silver Silver % availability in Service Window KPI.0.136.Gold TAM Gold Gold % availability in Service Window The availability measurement is monthly and calculated as: (Total time of service window of the measured period – time of outage based on Cegeka infrastructure monitoring during the measured period) / Total time of service window of the measured period) * 100

Section.name: Standard Service Billing
Section.Content: The service is billed on a monthly basis. ID Component Billing element BILLING UNIT SKU.0.L.136.L01 RedHat AMQ TAM Service RedHat AMQ TAM – Gold Per customer environment SKU.0.L.136.L02 RedHat AMQ TAM – Silver Per customer environment SKU.0.L.136.L03 RedHat AMQ TAM – Bronze Per customer environment Measurement The quantity of each billing element is determined on a monthly basis: Calculation / Billing The price(s) is/are determined in the pricing schedule where this Service is residing. The Service is billed per billing element:

Section.name: Optional Service Billing
Section.Content: n/a

Section.name: Service Request Billing
Section.Content: Changes are billed on a monthly basis. Changes as result of Pre-approved and normal Service requests are billed according to the defined prices in the pricing document of the related contact. Non-standard change requests are billed on T&M basis.

Section.name: Document control
Section.Content: Document version Date Author Notes DV1.0 27/09/2023 Johan Pauwels First version aligned with structural Service Description requirements. DV1.1 25/10/2024 Johan Pauwels Comments removed, document cleanup for PDF.
